todos.title: Replace scratch dict lookups with direct offsets in build_kernel
todos.micro_goal: Implement the scheduled design contract within this scope: In KernelBuilder.build_kernel, replace self.scratch[v] and self.scratch_const calls with pre-computed integer offsets stored in local variables, eliminating dictionary lookups during instruction building.
todos.implementation_hint: repeated_active_task_drift_requires_contract_rewrite: CODE kept violating or over-broadening this active task contract. Rewrite the task as a smaller executable probe, or retire it in favor of a different runnable task.

risk_evidence.quote: replace self.scratch[v] and self.scratch_const calls with pre-computed integer offsets stored in local variables
risk_evidence.explanation: This is a local edit within a single method, changing how addresses are stored and accessed but not changing the control flow or instruction semantics.

todos.probe_plan: Modify build_kernel to store addresses in a list instead of a dict, and update references to use list indexing.

probe_diff_contract.observation: The instruction list generated by build_kernel should remain identical in content, only the internal storage mechanism changes.

invariant_evidence: The scratch addresses are still allocated sequentially and stored in a list, preserving the mapping.
todos.edit_scope: In KernelBuilder.build_kernel, replace self.scratch[v] and self.scratch_const calls with pre-computed integer offsets stored in local variables, eliminating dictionary lookups during instruction building.
todos.correctness_rationale: The change is a pure refactoring of the storage mechanism for scratch addresses, not changing the logic of address allocation or usage.
todos.fallback_plan: Revert to the original dict-based implementation if CYCLES do not improve or tests fail.
todos.rollback_or_shrink_plan: Revert the changes to build_kernel to the original dict-based implementation.

todos.title: Pack adjacent debug slots in KernelBuilder.build
todos.micro_goal: Implement the scheduled design contract within this scope: In KernelBuilder.build, modify the loop to pack consecutive debug engine slots into single bundles, respecting SLOT_LIMITS['debug']=64.

risk_evidence.quote: modify the loop to pack consecutive debug engine slots into single bundles
risk_evidence.explanation: This is a local edit that only changes how slots are grouped, not the execution order or data flow of non-debug instructions.

todos.probe_plan: Implement a simple packing strategy that groups consecutive debug slots into bundles of up to 64.

probe_diff_contract.observation: The number of bundles should decrease, and CYCLES should decrease.

invariant_evidence: debug slots do not modify scratch or memory
packing debug slots does not affect other engines
todos.edit_scope: In KernelBuilder.build, modify the loop to pack consecutive debug engine slots into single bundles, respecting SLOT_LIMITS['debug']=64.

validator.failure_condition: Metric not found with regex: CYCLES:\s*(\d+) or exit code != 0

todos.correctness_rationale: Debug slots are side-effect free in the simulator (they only perform comparisons and assertions). Packing them does not change execution order or data dependencies. The simulator executes all slots in a bundle simultaneously, and since debug slots have no side effects, packing them is safe.
todos.fallback_plan: Revert build to the original implementation if correctness fails or cycles do not decrease.
todos.rollback_or_shrink_plan: Shrink to a single guarded branch for one instruction type (e.g., ALU) and revert if it fails.

todos.probe_plan: Add three local variables for zero_const, one_const, two_const before the loop and replace the corresponding self.scratch_const calls in the loop body.

invariant_evidence: scratch_const is idempotent for the same value

todos.title: Cache self.scratch dict reference in build_kernel

risk_evidence.quote: cache self.scratch in a local variable before the loop and use it directly instead of repeated attribute access
risk_evidence.explanation: This is a local edit that only changes how the scratch dict is accessed, not changing bundle structure or data flow.

todos.probe_plan: Add a local variable 'scratch = self.scratch' before the loop and replace all self.scratch references in build_kernel with 'scratch'.

probe_diff_contract.observation: The number of self.scratch attribute accesses should decrease.

invariant_evidence: self.scratch is not modified during the loop except by alloc_scratch which is called before the loop for init_vars
todos.edit_scope: In build_kernel, cache self.scratch in a local variable before the loop and use it directly instead of repeated attribute access.

validator.failure_condition: CYCLES line not found or exit code != 0 or pytest fails

todos.correctness_rationale: Caching a reference to a mutable object does not change its contents or behavior, only reduces attribute lookup overhead.
todos.fallback_plan: Revert to self.scratch if CYCLES line is not parseable or tests fail.
todos.rollback_or_shrink_plan: Remove the local variable assignment and restore all self.scratch references.
